pitch.title: I build websites & web apps that grow revenue.
pitch.description: One‑month delivery: strategy, development, integrations, launch & analytics. Focus on speed, conversion and ROI.

branding.description: From ideation to deployment, I build high-performance applications that scale businesses.

list.label: cases
list.title: Real projects with measurable results
list.description: Each project solves specific business goals with clear tasks and outcomes.

detail.keyMetricsSubtitle: Specific performance indicators of the project

invite.title: Invite — invitations and event management platform
invite.description: A multi-tenant SaaS platform for creating digital invitations and managing events in Kazakhstan: birthdays, weddings and toi, corporate events. A ready invitation with RSVP, guest seating, wishlist and QR check-in, plus a directory of vendors and venues. A Next.js 14 + NestJS monorepo with tRPC, session-based Lucia v3 auth and FREE/PRO/BUSINESS plans.
invite.shortDescription: Digital invitations SaaS with RSVP, QR check-in and ticketing
invite.imageAlt: Invite — invitations and event management platform
goals: Launch a digital invitations SaaS with RSVP and QR check-in
Achieve end-to-end type safety via tRPC + Zod with no codegen
Integrate local payment providers (Kaspi, Halyk, TipTop, Stripe)
Deliver multi-channel notifications: Email, SMS, WhatsApp, Telegram
tasks: Design a monorepo: Next.js 14 frontend + NestJS backend + shared Zod types
Implement session-based auth on Lucia v3 with a Prisma adapter
Build guest management: RSVP, seating, +1, contact import (.vcf), VIP statuses
Develop ticketing and billing with a grace-period model and FREE/PRO/BUSINESS plans
Add a lazy-loaded Fabric.js invitation editor and Mapbox maps
Connect a Telegram bot for guest notifications and RSVP collection
Set up CI/CD: build images in GHCR and deploy to a VPS
results: Over 12,000 invitations created on the platform
A catalog of 50+ ready templates and a UI in three languages (RU/KZ/EN)
Live billing with local payment providers and a grace period
457 backend tests and 93 frontend tests, Lighthouse ~90
Automatic deploys on push to main via GitHub Actions and GHCR

rals.title: RALS — furniture store with built-in CRM
rals.description: An online furniture and interior store with a built-in CRM for the sales team. Migrated from a legacy WordPress/WooCommerce site to a modern full-stack: a catalog with filters and Meilisearch search, Kaspi and Halyk payments, a kanban deals pipeline, 360° customer profiles and WhatsApp communications. Deployed on a VPS in Kazakhstan for data residency.
rals.shortDescription: Furniture e-commerce + CRM: catalog, Kaspi payments, sales pipeline and WhatsApp
rals.imageAlt: RALS — furniture store with built-in CRM
goals: Migrate the store from WooCommerce to a modern full-stack without losing the catalog
Unite the storefront and the sales CRM in a single system
Integrate local payments: Kaspi Pay/QR with installments and Halyk ePay
Ensure data residency — self-hosted on a VPS in Kazakhstan
tasks: Design a Next.js 14 + NestJS + tRPC + Prisma monorepo
Build a catalog with product variants, filters and Meilisearch search
Develop an admin panel: products, brands, categories, orders, PDF quotes
Build the CRM: kanban pipeline, leads, deals, tasks and 360° customer profiles
Integrate Kaspi and Halyk, promo codes and marketing feed generation
Set up a 5-role RBAC and analytics (GA4, Yandex Metrika, Meta/TikTok Pixel)
Deploy the infrastructure: Docker Compose + Nginx + Let’s Encrypt on a VPS
results: A single system: online store and the sales CRM
A catalog with 400+ product variants and fast Meilisearch search
Live payments via Kaspi and Halyk with installment support
Marketing feeds for Google Shopping, Meta and Yandex
Self-hosted deploy on a VPS with automated CI/CD via GitHub Actions

okiyo.title: OKIYO — eyewear store on Next.js + Payload CMS
okiyo.description: A Japanese-style eyewear online store with an integrated Payload CMS admin that lets non-technical staff manage products, categories, prices and leads without code. A catalog with color variants and specifications, lead capture and CMS-managed content and SEO. Deployed on a shared host under Caddy with automatic TLS.
okiyo.shortDescription: Eyewear storefront on Next.js 16 + Payload CMS with code-free catalog management
okiyo.imageAlt: OKIYO — eyewear store on Next.js and Payload CMS
goals: Build an eyewear storefront with catalog management without a developer
Give non-technical staff full control over content and SEO
Implement lead capture and processing right in the admin
Deploy the project on a shared host with automatic TLS
tasks: Build the app on Next.js 16 (App Router) + React 19 + Tailwind CSS v4
Embed headless Payload CMS 3 at /admin within the same app
Design a product model with color variants, specifications and stock
Build the storefront: paginated catalog, category and featured sections, hero
Create a lead form and a lead management panel with statuses
Move branding, contacts, socials and SEO into CMS-managed settings
Deploy via Docker Compose with Caddy (TLS) and PostgreSQL 16
results: A storefront launched with fully self-service content management
Staff manage products, prices and leads without involving a developer
A flexible catalog model: color variants, stock, specifications
CMS-managed SEO fields and homepage content
Deployed on a shared host with automatic TLS via Caddy

egemen.title: Egemen Qazaqstan — a new platform for the newspaper
egemen.description: Migrating the leading national newspaper “Egemen Qazaqstan” from a legacy Laravel 5.8/PHP 7.1/MySQL stack to a modern multi-tenant platform built on Next.js 16 + NestJS 11 + PostgreSQL 16. Hierarchical rubrics, RBAC with granular permissions, change auditing, newsletters, daily PDF generation and real-time updates over WebSocket.
egemen.shortDescription: Migrating Egemen Qazaqstan from Laravel to Next.js/NestJS and PostgreSQL
goals: Migrate from Laravel 5.8/PHP 7.1 to NestJS 11/Next.js 16 without data loss
Move from MySQL to PostgreSQL 16 preserving the full publication history
Introduce a multi-tenant architecture with per-edition data isolation
Ensure SEO compatibility and keep search rankings
tasks: Audit the legacy Laravel: routes, models, controllers, MySQL schema
Design a MySQL → PostgreSQL 16 migration with type mapping
Implement NestJS modules and a Next.js frontend with SSR for SEO
Build hierarchical rubrics with visual customization
Introduce RBAC, change auditing (before/after) and newsletters
Add daily PDF generation and real-time updates over WebSocket
Containerize the stack: web + api + PostgreSQL + Redis + MinIO
results: A modern platform replacing legacy Laravel 5.8
Publication history migrated to PostgreSQL 16 without loss
A multi-tenant architecture with per-edition data isolation
RBAC with granular permissions and full change auditing
Newsletters, a daily PDF edition and real-time updates

kazpravda.title: Kazakhstanskaya Pravda — news portal migration
kazpravda.description: A pilot migration of the national newspaper “Kazakhstanskaya Pravda” from a legacy Django 3.2 stack to a unified Next.js 16 + NestJS 11 + PostgreSQL 16 platform. Bilingual content (kk/ru), full-text search, a publishing workflow (draft/scheduled/published), likes, premium-content subscriptions and change auditing.
kazpravda.shortDescription: Moving Kazakhstanskaya Pravda from Django to Next.js/NestJS with search and subscriptions
goals: Migrate the portal from legacy Django 3.2 to a modern TypeScript stack
Preserve bilingual content (Kazakh and Russian)
Introduce full-text search and advanced filtering
Implement subscriptions and premium access to materials
tasks: Audit the legacy Django portal and the PostgreSQL schema
Develop NestJS modules and a Next.js frontend with SSR
Implement a publishing workflow: draft → scheduled → published
Add full-text search and filtering by rubric
Introduce likes for authenticated and anonymous readers
Implement premium-content subscriptions and edition branding
Set up change auditing and SEO metadata with tags
results: A modern portal replacing legacy Django 3.2
Bilingual content with full-text search and filtering
A publishing workflow with scheduled releases
Subscriptions and premium access to content
Change auditing and SEO optimization on a unified platform

kazgazeta.title: Qazaq Gazetteri — a holding of 13 publications
kazgazeta.description: Migrating the “Qazaq Gazetteri” publishing holding from legacy Laravel 7.30 to a unified multi-tenant platform on Next.js 16 + NestJS 11. A single application serves 13 publications (Ana tili, Aqiqat, Aq Jelken, Baldyrgan, Ulan, Mysl, Uigur Avazi and more) via dynamic Host-header routing with individual branding and settings per subdomain.
kazgazeta.shortDescription: A platform for 13 Qazaq Gazetteri publications with multi-domain routing
kazgazeta.imageAlt: Qazaq Gazetteri — a holding of 13 publications
goals: Migrate the holding’s 13 publications from legacy Laravel 7.30 onto one platform
Serve all subdomains from a single application via the Host header
Give each publication its own branding, themes and navigation
Support multilingual content (Kazakh, Russian, Uyghur)
tasks: Design a multi-tenant model with a shared news pool
Implement Host-based routing for 13 subdomains in one Web container
Store per-subdomain settings in the database with a Redis cache
Build hierarchical rubrics with visual customization
Implement filtering, pagination and a publishing workflow
Introduce auditing and real-time updates over WebSocket
Deploy via Docker/Dokploy behind a reverse proxy (Nginx/Caddy)
results: 13 publications on one platform instead of legacy Laravel 7.30
All subdomains routed from a single application by Host
Individual branding and settings for each publication
Multilingual content: Kazakh, Russian and Uyghur
A shared news pool with per-edition data isolation

nativetravel.title: NativeTravel — an AI-first travel marketplace
nativetravel.description: A two-sided marketplace of local experiences with AI personalization: tours, restaurants, events and activities. A multi-tenant architecture supporting multiple cities and brands, semantic search on pgvector, AI recommendations, translations and moderation via Claude. A Fastify + tRPC backend and a Next.js 15 + React 19 frontend. The project is in active development.
nativetravel.shortDescription: An AI-first marketplace of local experiences with pgvector semantic search
goals: Build a two-sided marketplace of local experiences with AI matching
Implement multi-tenancy for multiple cities and brands
Introduce semantic search and recommendations on embeddings
Keep the app working even without AI keys (deterministic fallback)
tasks: Design a monorepo: a Fastify + tRPC v11 backend and a Next.js 15 frontend
Model the domain (~45 Prisma models, 27+ modules)
Wire up PostgreSQL 15 + pgvector for embeddings and semantic search
Integrate Claude (chat, translations, moderation, vision) and OpenAI (embeddings)
Implement bookings, payments (Stripe/Kaspi) and host payouts
Build a partner REST API for channels (OTA) with Bearer auth
Set up deployment via Docker Compose behind a Caddy reverse proxy
results: Ready infrastructure: monorepo, design system, i18n and an AI layer
Semantic search and recommendations on pgvector embeddings
Multi-tenancy: multiple cities, custom domains, per-tenant AI budgets
A partner REST API for OTAs and channel managers
A deterministic stub mode — the app runs without AI keys

zakyat-kz.title: ZAKYAT.KZ — Platform for Good Deeds
zakyat-kz.description: Independent online platform for targeted assistance, built on principles of trust, transparency and manual moderation. Enables people in need to directly connect with donors without intermediaries or money collection.
zakyat-kz.shortDescription: Platform for targeted assistance with manual moderation and transparency
goals: Create a transparent platform for targeted assistance without intermediaries
Ensure manual moderation of all requests for safety
Eliminate money collection and third-party involvement
Implement filtering and randomization system for requests
tasks: Develop request submission system with data validation
Create moderation panel with access rights (moderator/administrator)
Implement public request catalog with filtering by district and assistance type
Configure request display randomization for fairness
Implement request status system (pending/verified/rejected/assistance received)
Create logging for all moderator actions
Implement spam protection (1 request per day per phone number)
Configure SEO optimization and contact protection from indexing
Develop Telegram bot for moderators (Phase 2)
Add "I helped" button with spam protection (Phase 2)
results: Launched platform with full request moderation cycle
Implemented role system with access rights separation
Deployed spam protection and automatic data validation
Created transparent catalog with filtering and randomization
Configured logging system for tracking all actions
Ensured contact security through robots.txt and noindex

amiray-kz.title: AMIRAY.KZ — Digital Agency Website
amiray-kz.description: Corporate website for a digital agency from Almaty. Built on Next.js/TypeScript stack with server-side rendering, animations and full responsiveness. Implemented service pages, portfolio and CRM integration.
amiray-kz.shortDescription: Corporate website for an IT agency on Next.js with animations and SSR
goals: Build a modern corporate website with high loading speed
Implement service pages with detailed descriptions and pricing
Ensure SEO optimization to attract clients from search
Integrate contact form with the agency CRM system
tasks: Develop design system and component library
Implement server-side rendering (SSR) for key pages
Create animated sections using Framer Motion
Configure SEO metadata, structured data and sitemap
Integrate contact form with notification dispatch
Set up deployment via Docker/AWS with CI/CD pipeline
results: Site launched with Lighthouse Performance score 95+
Implemented 8+ service pages with detailed descriptions
SEO optimization configured for key search queries
First screen load time — under 1.5 seconds
Request system deployed with automated notifications

independent.description: Hobby became profession. End‑to‑end delivery from design to launch. Next.js, TypeScript, CRM/1C/Kaspi integrations. Focus on conversion and performance.

philosophy.title: Character & Thinking
philosophy.p1: I'm a systems thinker and strategist by nature. I'm interested in mechanisms — how processes, people, and structures work. I don't seek public visibility, but I care about influence — through the architecture of solutions, not words.
philosophy.p2: I don't believe in coincidences as magic. I believe in patterns and in the ability to build environments where the right outcomes become probable.
philosophy.p3: There's a stoic part of me — I try to maintain inner stability regardless of circumstances. I'm not prone to dramatizing and prefer analyzing reactions.
philosophy.p4: I'm not fully defined by labels and roles — and I consider that normal. I don't want to freeze into a ready-made identity. What matters to me is growth and depth of understanding.
philosophy.highlight1: My goal is not to be noticeable.
philosophy.highlight2: My goal is to be precise.
philosophy.p5: I value clarity, structure, independent thinking, and inner harmony. I work not for approval, but for the quality and durability of the result.
philosophy.p6: I don't control reality — I study its laws and try to act in accordance with them.

story.title: How I became a developer
story.p1: Before development, I worked in the quasi‑public sector — at <kaz>Kazatomprom</kaz>, <alt>Altynalmas</alt> and <sk>Samruk‑Kazyna</sk>. This was experience in large organizations with strict requirements and responsibility.
story.p2: Over time, I realized I wanted to do what brings measurable outcomes. I burned out from bureaucracy and processes that didn’t help business. I decided to turn development into my main occupation — a hobby into a profession.
story.p3: This path taught me responsibility, work on large projects and how business processes should function in practice. Now every project is a tool to achieve goals.
story.p4: I work for results and value time and quality. Your project is an opportunity to create something that truly helps.

cookieConsent.title: We use cookies
cookieConsent.description: We use cookies to improve site performance, analytics, and content personalization.

necessary.title: Necessary Cookies
necessary.description: These cookies are essential for the website to function and cannot be disabled. They are usually set in response to your actions, such as setting privacy preferences, logging in, or filling out forms.

analytics.title: Analytics Cookies
analytics.description: These cookies help us understand how visitors interact with the website by collecting and reporting information anonymously. This allows us to improve site performance.

marketing.title: Marketing Cookies
marketing.description: These cookies are used to track visitors across different websites. The goal is to display relevant ads and encourage interest in products and services.

introduction.title: Introduction
introduction.content: This Privacy Policy describes how we collect, use, and protect your personal information when using our website. We are committed to protecting your privacy and complying with applicable data protection laws.

dataCollection.title: Data Collection
dataCollection.content: We collect the following types of information:

items.name: Name and contact information (when filling out forms)
items.usage: Website usage data (through cookies and analytics)

cookies.title: Cookie Usage
cookies.content: We use cookies to improve website functionality. You can manage cookie settings through the consent panel.

necessary.title: Necessary Cookies
necessary.description: These cookies are essential for the website to function and cannot be disabled. They provide basic functionality and security.

analytics.title: Analytics Cookies
analytics.description: These cookies help us understand how visitors use the website by collecting information anonymously. This allows us to improve user experience.

marketing.title: Marketing Cookies
marketing.description: These cookies are used to track the effectiveness of advertising campaigns and personalize content.

dataUsage.title: Data Usage
dataUsage.content: We use collected data for the following purposes:

items.communication: Communicating with you regarding your project
items.analytics: Analyzing website usage to improve service

dataProtection.title: Data Protection
dataProtection.content: We implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect your personal data from unauthorized access, alteration, disclosure, or destruction. Data is stored on secure servers and transmitted through encrypted channels.

userRights.title: Your Rights

items.portability: Receive a copy of your data in a structured format

contact.title: Contact
contact.content: If you have questions about this Privacy Policy or wish to exercise your rights, please contact us at:
